codeReview.summary: The ContextCondensation implementation is correct and meets requirements (75% threshold, auto-compact, priority preservation), but this feature was NOT implemented by worker session 80c83202 - it was pre-existing in commit 3a8c017. The handoff's commitId '8ad9098' corresponds to RefactorerAgent enhancement, NOT context condensation. The worker merely verified an existing implementation rather than implementing the feature.

issues.description: Handoff claims commitId '8ad9098' implemented context condensation, but git show confirms 8ad9098 is 'feat(orchestrator): enhance RefactorerAgent with refactoring analysis' - no context condensation changes present
Worker did not implement the feature - ContextCondensation was introduced in commit 3a8c017 ('feat: complete Rust backend skeleton'), not by this worker session
Feature specifies skillName 'rust-worker' which does not exist in .factory/skills/ - worker correctly fell back to mission-worker-base

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature's skillName references 'rust-worker' which doesn't exist in .factory/skills/. Worker had to deviate and use 'mission-worker-base' instead. This mismatch between features.json skillName and actual available skills could cause confusion for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows skillName: 'rust-worker' but ls .factory/skills/ shows only 'worker' directory exists

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff format includes a 'commitId' field intended to reference the worker's implementation commit, but the worker used the HEAD commit (which was pre-existing work) rather than creating a new commit for any changes made. This makes it unclear what the worker actually changed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: commitId '8ad9098' in handoff matches current HEAD but git show --stat shows no context condensation changes in this commit

sharedStateObservations.observation: No knowledge gaps identified - the implementation was pre-existing and worker correctly identified it. However, if this feature was meant to be NEW implementation work (not verification), there would be a significant gap between expectation and reality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ContextCondensation struct exists at lines 1281-1283 in agents.rs with correct 75% threshold, introduced in original skeleton commit

summary: Feature status is FAIL due to process violations: worker did not implement context condensation (pre-existing in commit 3a8c017), and handoff's commitId (8ad9098) incorrectly attributes unrelated RefactorerAgent work to this feature. The actual ContextCondensation implementation in agents.rs:1281-1355 is CORRECT: it monitors context via utilization(), triggers condensation at 75% threshold via needs_condensation(), and auto-compacts via condense() with priority-based preservation. Worker correctly noted skill 'rust-worker' doesn't exist and used mission-worker-base instead. Tests pass confirming correct behavior.
